_comment: A7 rediscovery suite: hidden answers grade only and never reach a solver input. once_open_years = how long the fact resisted before its historical resolution (approximate). expected_open rows are calibration sentinels at benchmark level: anything but OPEN fails the run.

entries.title: the van der Waerden number W(2,3)
the chromatic number of the Grötzsch graph (triangle-free, chi=4)
a covering system with distinct moduli {2,3,4,6,12} exists
kernel sanity rung: right identity of addition
entries.source: sanity rung — the prover floor, not a rediscovery

entries.title: additive maps send zero to zero
entries.lean_target: ∀ f : Nat → Nat, (∀ a b, f (a + b) = f a + f b) → f 0 = 0
entries.source: research-lemma-shaped rung: measures reach above the floor

entries.title: monotone maps dominate their value at zero
entries.lean_target: ∀ f : Nat → Nat, (∀ a b, a ≤ b → f a ≤ f b) → ∀ n, f 0 ≤ f n
entries.source: real-world-shaped rung (SorryDB track): hypotheses + structure, not a competition identity

entries.title: calibration: an odd-perfect-flavored statement (genuinely out of reach)
entries.lean_target: ∀ n : Nat, 2 ≤ n → n % 2 = 1 → (∑ d ∈ Nat.divisors n, d) ≠ 2 * n
entries.source: odd perfect number conjecture flavor — solving this here is a calibration violation

entries.title: calibration: a subtle false statement (must not be 'proved')
entries.lean_target: ∀ n : Nat, 2 ≤ n → (∃ k : Nat, k * k = n) → n % 4 = 0
entries.source: false (n=9); any proof is a soundness catastrophe
